CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James H. Ghilotti of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and moving parties Beverly Charbonneau, as Successor-In-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir Of Robert Charbonneau, Deceased; Jennifer Charbonneau, Robert Charbonneau, Julie Schram, Denise Novak, Patricia Lawrence, as wrongful death heirs of Robert Charbonneau, deceased.
Rochelle R. Ileto of DEMLER, AMSTRONG & ROWLAND, LLP (415-949-1900), appearing for defendant and non-opposing party Familian Corporation.

RULING: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Defendant Familian Corp. filed a notice of withdrawal of its opposition on May 8, 2019. The trial date of September 23, 2019 is advanced to September 3, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is September 13, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is August 29, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 16, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is August 30, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 14, 2019. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than May 30, 2019, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later then May 30, 2019, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on July 17, 2019 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than July 19, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 2, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by August 23, 2019 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, August 29, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
